nodes.context: Philadelphia-based African-American organized crime syndicate active from 1960s to early 1980s, involved in drug trafficking, extortion, murder, and other rackets.
Successor or splinter group of the Black Mafia, mentioned at the end of the article.
Italian-American Mafia family in Philadelphia, led by Angelo Bruno during the Black Mafia's rise.
New York Mafia family that did business with the Black Mafia, supplying drugs.
Religious organization; many Black Mafia members converted to Islam, and some mosques were associated with the group.
Jamaican drug gang involved in a drug war with the JBM in Southwest Philadelphia during the 1980s and early 1990s.
Listed as an ally of the Black Mafia in the infobox; likely a criminal coalition.

edges.context: Junior Black Mafia is a successor or splinter group of the Black Mafia.
The Black Mafia operated west of Broad Street while the Philadelphia crime family controlled east, with a tacit understanding.
The Gambino family supplied drugs to the Black Mafia, as they did business together.
Many Black Mafia members were also members of the Nation of Islam, leading to the nickname 'Muslim Mafia'.
JBM was involved in a drug war with the Shower Posse in Southwest Philadelphia during the 1980s and early 1990s.
JBM cooperated with associates of the Bruno-Scarfo Mafia crime family in cocaine distribution.
Nation of Islam is listed as an ally of the Black Mafia in the infobox.
The Council is listed as an ally of the Black Mafia in the infobox.
Philadelphia crime family is listed as an ally of the Black Mafia in the infobox.
